zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Berkeley於1305年11月5日出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊時，她的父親Sir Maurice Berkeley 2nd Baron Berkeley34歲，母親Eva la Zouche Baroness Berkeley30歲。她於1328年6月2日與Robert de Clifford在英國格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1362年7月25日在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德吉可比 斯蒂芬享年56歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de Berkeley于1305年11月5日出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊时，她的父亲Sir Maurice Berkeley 2nd Baron Berkeley34岁，母亲Eva la Zouche Baroness Berkeley30岁。她于1328年6月2日与Robert de Clifford在英国格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1362年7月25日在英国英格兰維斯特莫藍德吉可比 斯蒂芬享年56岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Berkeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or more of the numerous places so named, such as Berkeley in Gloucestershire, named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Scottish Barclay .
Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of   Berkowitz  .

variants.name: Barclay
meanings: Scottish: habitational name of English origin, from any of several places so called, such as Berkley in Somerset or Berkeley in Gloucestershire, both named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The surname was brought to Scotland from southwestern England in the 12th century; Walter de Berchelai or Berkelai was Chamberlain of Scotland in 1165. It was long assumed that they were from the family that held Berkeley Castle in Gloucestershire, but the historian G. W. S. Barrow (1973, The Kingdom of the Scots) adduced evidence that they are more probably from Berkley in Somerset.

variants.name: Berkley
meanings: English: variant of   Berkeley  . Compare Scottish   Barclay  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Berkeley was born on 5 November 1305, in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Sir Maurice Berkeley 2nd Baron Berkeley, was 34 and her mother, Eva la Zouche Baroness Berkeley, was 30. She married Robert de Clifford on 2 June 1328, in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 25 July 1362, in Kirkby Stephen, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56, and was buried in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Berkeley est née le 5 novembre 1305, à Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Maurice Berkeley 2nd Baron Berkeley, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Eva la Zouche Baroness Berkeley, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Robert de Clifford le 2 juin 1328, à Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 25 juillet 1362, à Kirkby Stephen, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Berkeley am 5. November 1305 in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Maurice Berkeley 2nd Baron Berkeley 34 und ihre Mutter Eva la Zouche Baroness Berkeley 30. Sie hat am 2. Juni 1328 in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England Robert de Clifford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 25. Juli 1362 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Kirkby Stephen, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Berkeleyが1305年11月5日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアバーケリーで生まれた時，父Sir Maurice Berkeley 2nd Baron Berkeleyは34歳，母Eva la Zouche Baroness Berkeleyは30歳であった。1328年6月2日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアバーケリーでRobert de Cliffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1362年7月25日にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドキルクビー ステフンで56歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアバーケリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Brough East Riding Yorkshire England: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 브루그h 인 스키프시
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Berkeley(이)가 1305년 11월 5일 영국 그루스스터실르 버켈리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Maurice Berkeley 2nd Baron Berkeley(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eva la Zouche Baroness Berkeley(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1328년 6월 2일 영국 그루스스터실르 버켈리에서 Robert de Clifford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1362년 7월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 커크비 스티븐에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 버켈리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Berkeley nasceu em 5 de novembro de 1305, em Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Maurice Berkeley 2nd Baron Berkeley, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Eva la Zouche Baroness Berkeley, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert de Clifford em 2 de junho de 1328, em Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 25 de julho de 1362, em Kirkby Stephen, Westmorland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 56 anos, e foi sepultada em Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.Brough East Riding Yorkshire England: броую ин скипсеа, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Berkeley родилась 5 ноября 1305 г. в местности биркели, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Maurice Berkeley 2nd Baron Berkeley было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Eva la Zouche Baroness Berkeley, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Clifford 2 июня 1328 г. в местности биркели, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 25 июля 1362 г. в местности киркби стифен, вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности биркели, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel de Berkeley nació el 5 de noviembre de 1305, en Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Maurice Berkeley 2nd Baron Berkeley, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Eva la Zouche Baroness Berkeley, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Robert de Clifford el 2 de junio de 1328, en Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 25 de julio de 1362, en Kirkby Stephen, Westmorland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultada en Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
